mnemonics.title: Types of Pollution
mnemonics.content: FANS - F(Fossil fuels/Air), A(Agricultural/Water), N(Noise), S(Solid/Soil)

mnemonics.title: Air Pollutants
mnemonics.content: S N O P - S(SO2), N(NOx), O(Ozone), P(PM, CO, Pb)

mnemonics.title: 3 Rs of Waste Management
mnemonics.content: R-R-R: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle - Remember to Reduce first, then Reuse, finally Recycle as last option

acronyms.usage: PM2.5 and PM10 are key air pollution indicators measured in μg/m³
BOD measures organic pollution in water - higher BOD means more pollution
Indian nodal agency for environmental protection and pollution control
Sets international air quality guidelines for protecting public health
Gases like CO2, CH4, N2O that trap heat and cause global warming
Ozone-depleting substances used in refrigerators and air conditioners

visualTips.visualization: Picture a city with factories (industry), vehicles (transport), and buildings (construction) all releasing pollutants into the sky
Imagine a food pyramid: small fish eat plankton (low pollution), big fish eat small fish (medium), humans eat big fish (high concentration)
Picture a lake turning green with algae, then fish dying, and water becoming murky - all from excess nutrients
Think of Earth as a greenhouse with glass windows - heat enters but can't escape, warming the interior

analogies.analogy: Just as an air filter captures dust in an AC, our lungs filter the air we breathe - polluted air clogs and damages this filter like a dirty AC filter
Just as toxins accumulate in our body causing disease, waste accumulation in the environment causes 'sickness' in the ecosystem
Pollution is like taking a loan from nature - eventually we must 'pay it back' through environmental disasters and health costs
